devanagari: इंद्रमखभङ्ग । श्रीशुक उवाच । भगवानपि तत्रैव बलदेवेन संयुतः । अपश्यत् निवसन् गोपान् इन्द्रयागकृतोद्यमान् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: iṃdramakhabhaṅga śrīśuka uvāca bhagavānapi tatraiva baladevena saṃyutaḥ apaśyat nivasan gopān indrayāgakṛtodyamān

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
While living in that very locality (viz. Vṛndāvana, Vraja) in the company of Balarāma, even the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa feigned as if he did not notice the cowherds engaged in preparation for the Indra-yāga (worship of the rain-god Indra).[1]

devanagari: तदभिज्ञोऽपि भगवान् सर्वात्मा सर्वदर्शनः । प्रश्रयावनतोऽपृच्छत् वृद्धान् नन्दपुरोगमान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tadabhijño praśrayāvanato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: Despite his thorough knowledge of it, the omniscient glorious Lord who was the indwelling Soul of all beings, with great humility, enquired of elderly Gopas of whom Nanda was prominent.

devanagari: कथ्यतां मे पितः कोऽयं सम्भ्रमो व उपागतः । किं फलं कस्य चोद्देशः केन वा साध्यते मखः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kathyatāṃ me pitaḥ ko kiṃ phalaṃ kasya coddeśaḥ kena vā sādhyate makhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: “May it be told to me, Oh father, what occasion for your hurry and flurry has arrived? (If it be a sacrifice) what is the fruit or purpose of it? Who is regarded as competent to perform it? And with what materials is it to be performed?

devanagari: एतद्‍ब्रूहि महान् कामो मह्यं शुश्रूषवे पितः । न हि गोप्यं हि साधूनां कृत्यं सर्वात्मनामिह ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: etad‍brūhi mahān kāmo mahyaṃ śuśrūṣave pitaḥ na hi gopyaṃ hi sādhūnāṃ kṛtyaṃ sarvātmanāmiha

translation: I am desirous of hearing it, Oh father! Please do tell it to me who am eager to learn. For there is nothing worth concealing in the case of the righteous who look upon the selfsame soul as abiding in all beings also in the case of those who dp not discriminate between people (or things) as their own or as of others (and are of impartial outlook), have no friends, aliens or enemies, and have nothing to hide from others. But to those who retain this discrimination, the neutrals may be avoided as enemies, but a friend or a well-wisher has been called as one’s own self[2] (and as such, nothing should be kept secret from him. I, being such, should be confided with this sacrifice affair).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī explains: one should not keep everything secret from everybody. One should avoid a neutral party as an enemy, but not do so in the case of a friend or a well-wisher who is as good as one’s own-self.

devanagari: अस्त्यस्वपरदृष्टीनां अमित्रोदास्तविद्विषाम् । उदासीनोऽरिवद् वर्ज्य आत्मवत् सुहृदुच्यते ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: astyasvaparadṛṣṭīnāṃ amitrodāstavidviṣām udāsīno
translation: I am desirous of hearing it, Oh father! Please do tell it to me who am eager to learn. For there is nothing worth concealing in the case of the righteous who look upon the selfsame soul as abiding in all beings also in the case of those who dp not discriminate between people (or things) as their own or as of others (and are of impartial outlook), have no friends, aliens or enemies, and have nothing to hide from others. But to those who retain this discrimination, the neutrals may be avoided as enemies, but a friend or a well-wisher has been called as one’s own self[2] (and as such, nothing should be kept secret from him. I, being such, should be confided with this sacrifice affair).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī explains: one should not keep everything secret from everybody. One should avoid a neutral party as an enemy, but not do so in the case of a friend or a well-wisher who is as good as one’s own-self.

devanagari: ज्ञात्वाज्ञात्वा च कर्माणि जनोऽयमनुतिष्ठति । विदुषः कर्मसिद्धिः स्यात् तथा नाविदुषो भवेत् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: jñātvājñātvā ca karmāṇi jano viduṣaḥ karmasiddhiḥ syāt tathā nāviduṣo bhavet

translation: People perform actions with or without understanding the purpose or the fruit of the act. The ignorant persons do not accomplish the fruit of that action (to that extent) as people with full knowledge achieve.

devanagari: तत्र तावत् क्रियायोगो भवतां किं विचारितः । अथ वा लौकिकस्तन्मे पृच्छतः साधु भण्यताम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tāvat kriyāyogo bhavatāṃ kiṃ vicāritaḥ atha vā laukikastanme pṛcchataḥ sādhu bhaṇyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ praś class 6 verb], [ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]
[ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]

translation: May it be clearly explained to me who am inquisitive about it, as to whether your present course of action (viz. performance of sacrifice to Indra) has been undertaken after enquiring into the Śāstras or in consultation with friends or is it a mere blind traditional function in the ways of the world.

devanagari: श्रीनन्द उवाच । पर्जन्यो भगवान् इन्द्रो मेघास्तस्यात्ममूर्तयः । तेऽभिवर्षन्ति भूतानां प्रीणनं जीवनं पयः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnanda uvāca parjanyo bhagavān indro meghāstasyātmamūrtayaḥ te

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛṣ class 1 verb]

translation:
Nanda explained:
[3] The great god Indra is (the presiding deity of) the rainfall itself and clouds are his loving manifestations. They shower water which is the very source of delight[4] and life of all beings.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā quotes the famous verse which identifies the sun-god with the rain:
aṣṭau māsān nipītaṃ yad bhūmyāścodamayaṃ vasu /
sva-gobhir moktum ārebhe parjanyaḥ kālam āgate //
But he is under Indra’s control and as such, the presiding deity of rain and Indra are identified. The clouds are like so many bodies of Indra. They shower water which is so vital to the life of the people and are a source of strength and happiness to them.

Footnote [4]:
v.l.—prāṇanaṃ (Padaratnāvalī) “infusing life and activity”.

devanagari: तं तात वयमन्ये च वार्मुचां पतिमीश्वरम् । द्रव्यैस्तद् रेतसा सिद्धैः यजन्ते क्रतुभिर्नराः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tāta vayamanye ca vārmucāṃ patimīśvaram dravyaistad retasā siddhaiḥ yajante kratubhirnarāḥ
translation: Oh child! We and other people like us, worship[5] Indra, the ruler of clouds and controller of the world, by performing sacrifices with materials produced with his seminal discharge viz. rain-water.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
VT explains—Otherwise it will be ungratefulness. The 3 P. pl. yajante instead of Vayaṃ yajāmaḥ is used as Nanda felt abashed before Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तच्छेषेणोपजीवन्ति त्रिवर्गफलहेतवे । पुंसां पुरुषकाराणां पर्जन्यः फलभावनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: taccheṣeṇopajīvanti trivargaphalahetave puṃsāṃ puruṣakārāṇāṃ parjanyaḥ phalabhāvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb]

translation: It is on the remnants of the sacrifice that people subsist for achieving their three purposes in life (viz. dharma—righteousness, artha—affluence and worldly prosperity and kāma—enjoyment of pleasures). The rain-god is the real bestower of fruits to persons who exert in agriculture, industry etc.

devanagari: य एनं विसृजेत् धर्मं परम्पर्यागतं नरः । कामाल्लोभात् भयाद् द्वेषात् स वै नाप्नोति शोभनम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ya enaṃ visṛjet dharmaṃ paramparyāgataṃ naraḥ kāmāllobhāt bhayād dveṣāt sa vai nāpnoti śobhanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: He who, out of passion, greed, fear or hatred, gives up the customary religious rites handed down by tradition, does not attain his real good.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वचो निशम्य नन्दस्य तथान्येषां व्रजौकसाम् । इन्द्राय मन्युं जनयन् पितरं प्राह केशवः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vaco niśamya nandasya tathānyeṣāṃ vrajaukasām indrāya manyuṃ janayan pitaraṃ prāha keśavaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the explanatory speech of Nanda as well as that of other residents of Vraja, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) addressed his father with a view to provoking Indra’s wrath (and finally to discomfit him).[6]

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । कर्मणा जायते जन्तुः कर्मणैव विलीयते । सुखं दुःखं भयं क्षेमं कर्मणैवाभिपद्यते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca karmaṇā jāyate jantuḥ karmaṇaiva vilīyate sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ bhayaṃ kṣemaṃ karmaṇaivābhipadyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord said:
A being comes into existence only by its own past deeds (karma),[7] and passes away through the force of its own karmas. It is only through the instrumentality of one’s past actions, that the being experiences worldly pleasures, pain, fear or blessings (hereafter). (The favour or disfavour of a divinity is immaterial and irrelevant).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Padaratnāvalī Here karma means Nārāyaṇa as He is the agent of all activities (nārāyaṇa eva sarvasya kartetyabhiprekṣya sarvasya kāraṇaṃ karmaivetyāha /)

devanagari: अस्ति चेदीश्वरः कश्चित् फलरूप्यन्यकर्मणाम् । कर्तारं भजते सोऽपि न ह्यकर्तुः प्रभुर्हि सः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: asti cedīśvaraḥ kaścit phalarūpyanyakarmaṇām kartāraṃ bhajate so
translation: If there be any authority like the Supreme Ruler to dispense fruits of actions of others, and if he were to resort to confer fruits of the deeds on the doer of the actions, and he will have no control over him who abstains from committing any act.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: explains that this is a refutation of those who regard God as dependent on karmas. God is untouched by and above all karmas. He is the bestower of the fruit of an action to its doer.
Padaratnāvalī: If it is presumed that there is a Supreme Controller to dispense weal or woe as a result of the karma of a person, He will be a mere helper (sahāyabhūtaḥ) with no independence of His own.

devanagari: किमिन्द्रेणेह भूतानां स्वस्वकर्मानुवर्तिनाम् । अनीशेनान्यथा कर्तुं स्वभावविहितं नृणाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kimindreṇeha bhūtānāṃ svasvakarmānuvartinām anīśenānyathā kartuṃ svabhāvavihitaṃ nṛṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: What power can Indra exercise on beings who follow the course of their actions (and experience the resulting fruit)? He is incapable of modifying its destined course of action of human beings according to their own individual nature. (It is the Almighty God and not Indra who can do it.)

devanagari: स्वभावतन्त्रो हि जनः स्वभावमनुवर्तते । स्वभावस्थमिदं सर्वं सदेवासुरमानुषम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: svabhāvatantro hi janaḥ svabhāvamanuvartate svabhāvasthamidaṃ sarvaṃ sadevāsuramānuṣam
translation: People are the slaves of their Nature and follow their own individual natural disposition and proclivities. The whole world of gods, asuras and human beings thus has their being established in nature and follow it.[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Padaratnāvalī identifies Nature with Nārāyaṇa. The above verses refuse the power to secondary divinities like Indra who are mistaken for the Supreme Lord. It is Nārāyaṇa who is designated by the term svabhāva.

devanagari: देहानुच्चावचाञ्जन्तुः प्राप्योत् सृजति कर्मणा । शत्रुर्मित्रमुदासीनः कर्मैव गुरुरीश्वरः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: dehānuccāvacāñjantuḥ prāpyot sṛjati karmaṇā śatrurmitramudāsīnaḥ karmaiva gururīśvaraḥ
translation: It is through the force of past actions (karma) that a being comes by and casts off corporeal forms of a high or low order. It is (nothing but one’s Karma that assumes the role of) an enemy, a friend, an indifferent person, a preceptor or the Supreme Controller.

devanagari: तस्मात् संपूजयेत्कर्म स्वभावस्थः स्वकर्मकृत् । अन्जसा येन वर्तेत तदेवास्य हि दैवतम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt saṃpūjayetkarma svabhāvasthaḥ svakarmakṛt anjasā yena varteta tadevāsya hi daivatam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: [10] Hence abiding in one’s own nature (i.e. following one’s natural disposition) one should do one’s duty (according to one’s own class or position in life), and thus respect (lit. adore) it (one’s prescribed karma). That deed whereby one can lead a happy (and a successful) life is verily his deity.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Padaratnāvalī interprets karma as Nārāyaṇa and states: One should perform his duty prescribed as such by his Varṇa (caste) and āśrama (stage in life), and dedicate every thing to Nature i.e. God Nārāyaṇa who is the creator and supporter of the world and is involved in every religious rite. The name of any god whom a being worships (e.g. god Gaṇeśa), denotes as a matter of fact Lord Nārāyaṇa Himself.

devanagari: आजीव्यैकतरं भावं यस्त्वन्यमुपजीवति । न तस्माद् विन्दते क्षेमं जारान्नार्यसती यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ājīvyaikataraṃ bhāvaṃ yastvanyamupajīvati na tasmād vindate kṣemaṃ jārānnāryasatī yathā
translation:
[11] A person who, entertaining faith in one god offers services to another, does not attain happiness and welfare, just as an unchaste woman gratifying her paramour, (fails to get prosperity or happiness).
20(A). Brāhmaṇa should maintain his livelihood by the study and the teachings of the Vedas; the Kṣattriya by the protection of the earth; a person from the Vaiśya community, by means of vārtā (as explained in the next verse); and a Śūḍra by rendering service to the twice-born castes (mentioned above).[12]
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Verse No. 24 in Padaratnāvalī’s Text.

Footnote [12]:
Cf. Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.10. 16-31.

devanagari: वर्तेत ब्रह्मणा विप्रो राजन्यो रक्षया भुवः । वैश्यस्तु वार्तया जीवेत् शूद्रस्तु द्विजसेवया ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: varteta brahmaṇā vipro rājanyo rakṣayā bhuvaḥ vaiśyastu vārtayā jīvet śūdrastu dvijasevayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

devanagari: कृषिवाणिज्यगोरक्षा कुसीदं तूर्यमुच्यते । वार्ता चतुर्विधा तत्र वयं गोवृत्तयोऽनिशम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣivāṇijyagorakṣā kusīdaṃ tūryamucyate vārtā caturvidhā tatra vayaṃ govṛttayo
translation: Vāriā is four-fold: viz. agriculture, commerce, rearing of cattle and the fourth, usury. As far as we are concerned, cows (the cattle) have been the means of our subsistence.

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति स्थित्युत्पत्त्यन्तहेतवः । रजसोत्पद्यते विश्वं अन्योन्यं विविधं जगत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti sthityutpattyantahetavaḥ rajasotpadyate viśvaṃ anyonyaṃ vividhaṃ jagat
translation: The attributes called sattva, rajas, and tamas are causes of the maintenance, creation and dissolution of the Universe. It is through rajas that the universe of various kinds of things is created by the mutual union of man and woman.

devanagari: रजसा चोदिता मेघा वर्षन्त्यम्बूनि सर्वतः । प्रजास्तैरेव सिध्यन्ति महेन्द्रः किं करिष्यति ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: rajasā coditā meghā varṣantyambūni sarvataḥ prajāstaireva sidhyanti mahendraḥ kiṃ kariṣyati

translation: It is after being impelled by rajas that the clouds shower water all round. By means of these waters people accomplish their purpose (of growing food etc.). What has the great Indra to do with this?

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे चतुर्विंशोऽध्यायः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe caturviṃśo
translation: Father! We have no towns, no countries or territories, no villages or no houses to dwell. We are forever forest-dwellers, living in jungles and on mountains.

devanagari: न नः पुरोजनपदा न ग्रामा न गृहा वयम् । नित्यं वनौकसस्तात वनशैलनिवासिनः । तस्माद्‍गवां ब्राह्मणानां अद्रेश्चारभ्यतां मखः । य इंद्रयागसंभ...
english_devnagari: na naḥ purojanapadā na grāmā na gṛhā vayam nityaṃ vanaukasastāta vanaśailanivāsinaḥ tasmād‍gavāṃ brāhmaṇānāṃ adreścārabhyatāṃ makhaḥ ya iṃdrayāgasaṃbhārāḥ tairayaṃ sādhyatāṃ makhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: Therefore, let a sacrifice dedicated for the propitiation of cows, Brāhmaṇas and the mount (Govardhana) be instituted. And let this sacrifice be accomplished with the materials collected for the worship to Indra.

devanagari: पच्यन्तां विविधाः पाकाः सूपान्ताः पायसादयः । संयावापूपशष्कुल्यः सर्वदोहश्च गृह्यताम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: pacyantāṃ vividhāḥ pākāḥ sūpāntāḥ pāyasādayaḥ saṃyāvāpūpaśaṣkulyaḥ sarvadohaśca gṛhyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative middle third plural], [imperative passive third plural]

translation: Let different varieties of sweet dishes from rice, boiled in milk with sugar, down to boiled pulses and various puddings and cakes be prepared and all the milk collected from all the cows be brought together.

devanagari: हूयन्तामग्नयः सम्यग् ब्राह्मणैर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः । अन्नं बहुगुणं तेभ्यो देयं वो धेनुदक्षिणाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: hūyantāmagnayaḥ samyag brāhmaṇairbrahmavādibhiḥ annaṃ bahuguṇaṃ tebhyo deyaṃ vo dhenudakṣiṇāḥ
translation: Let sacrificial fires be properly fed with oblations by Brāhmaṇas who are well versed in the Vedas, and let various food preparations be given to them along with cows, as sacrificial fees.

devanagari: अन्येभ्यश्चाश्वचाण्डाल पतितेभ्यो यथार्हतः । यवसं च गवां दत्त्वा गिरये दीयतां बलिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: anyebhyaścāśvacāṇḍāla patitebhyo yathārhataḥ yavasaṃ ca gavāṃ dattvā giraye dīyatāṃ baliḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative plural from √ pat class 1 verb], [ablative plural from √ pat class 1 verb]
[dative plural from √ pat class 1 verb], [ablative plural from √ pat class 1 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: Food should also be distributed to all others upto the cāṇḍāla, the low castes and dogs as may be deemed proper. Cows should be supplied with grass and the above mentioned articles of food be offered as bali to the mount Govardhana.

devanagari: स्वलङ्कृता भुक्तवन्तः स्वनुलिप्ताः सुवाससः । प्रदक्षिणां च कुरुत गोविप्रानलपर्वतान् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: svalaṅkṛtā bhuktavantaḥ svanuliptāḥ suvāsasaḥ pradakṣiṇāṃ ca kuruta goviprānalaparvatān
translation: After taking your meals you put on your ornaments and painting yourselves with sandal-pastes etc. and putting on excellent clothes, circumambulate by the right side to cow, Brāhmaṇas, sacrificial fires and the Govardhana mountain.

devanagari: एतन्मम मतं तात क्रियतां यदि रोचते । अयं गोब्राह्मणाद्रीणां मह्यं च दयितो मखः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: etanmama mataṃ tāta kriyatāṃ yadi rocate ayaṃ gobrāhmaṇādrīṇāṃ mahyaṃ ca dayito makhaḥ

translation: This is my view and religious belief, Oh father. It may be adopted if it is approved of by you. This type of worship and sacrifice will be liked by cows, Brāhmaṇas, the mountain (Govardhana) itself and by me as well.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कालात्मना भगवता शक्रदर्पं जिघांसता । प्रोक्तं निशम्य नन्दाद्याः साध्वगृह्णन्त तद्वचः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca kālātmanā bhagavatā śakradarpaṃ jighāṃsatā proktaṃ niśamya nandādyāḥ sādhvagṛhṇanta tadvacaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the exposition of (the creed as elucidated by) the glorious Lord who, as the Time-Spirit was desirous of crushing the pride of Indra, the Gopas headed by Nanda approved of it with words, “Correct, very nice”.

devanagari: तथा च व्यदधुः सर्वं यथाऽह मधुसूदनः । वाचयित्वा स्वस्त्ययनं तद् द्रव्येण गिरिद्विजान् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā ca vyadadhuḥ sarvaṃ yathā vācayitvā svastyayanaṃ tad dravyeṇa giridvijān
translation: And they carried out everything as Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of the demon Madhu, instructed them. Having got recited the benedictory hymns (and the expiatory rites to avert evil) known as Svastyayana, they propitiated with great respect, the mountain (Govardhana) and Brāhmaṇas with those very materials (collected for Indra-yāga, the worship of and sacrifice to Indra) and offered grass to the kine. With their wealth of cattle in front of them, they circumambulated the mount (Govardhana) by keeping that mount always on their right.

devanagari: उपहृत्य बलीन् सम्यग् सर्वान् आदृता यवसं गवाम् । गोधनानि पुरस्कृत्य गिरिं चक्रुः प्रदक्षिणम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: upahṛtya balīn samyag sarvān ādṛtā yavasaṃ gavām godhanāni puraskṛtya giriṃ cakruḥ pradakṣiṇam
translation: And they carried out everything as Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of the demon Madhu, instructed them. Having got recited the benedictory hymns (and the expiatory rites to avert evil) known as Svastyayana, they propitiated with great respect, the mountain (Govardhana) and Brāhmaṇas with those very materials (collected for Indra-yāga, the worship of and sacrifice to Indra) and offered grass to the kine. With their wealth of cattle in front of them, they circumambulated the mount (Govardhana) by keeping that mount always on their right.

devanagari: अनांस्यनडुद्युक्तानि ते चारुह्य स्वलङ्कृताः । गोप्यश्च कृष्णवीर्याणि गायन्त्यः सद्विजाशिषः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: anāṃsyanaḍudyuktāni te cāruhya svalaṅkṛtāḥ gopyaśca kṛṣṇavīryāṇi gāyantyaḥ sadvijāśiṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: With the gracefully adorned Gopa women singing the exploits of Kṛṣṇa, and with Brāhmaṇas pronouncing benedictions, the cowherds who also wore excellent ornaments, rode their carts with bullocks yoked to them, and went round the mountain keeping it ever to their right.

devanagari: कृष्णस्त्वन्यतमं रूपं गोपविश्रम्भणं गतः । शैलोऽस्मीति ब्रुवन् भूरि बलिमादद् बृहद्वपुः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇastvanyatamaṃ rūpaṃ gopaviśrambhaṇaṃ gataḥ śailo
translation: In order so inspire confidence and belief in the Gopas, Kṛṣṇa, on his part assumed a totally different personality. Announcing that he is the mountain Govardhana itself, he with his gigantic body, consumed the enormous quantity of the food offered.[13]

devanagari: तस्मै नमो व्रजजनैः सह चक्रेऽत्मनाऽत्मने । अहो पश्यत शैलोऽसौ रूपी नोऽनुग्रहं व्यधात् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai namo vrajajanaiḥ saha cakre aho paśyata śailo

translation: Thereupon, along with the inhabitants of Vraja Kṛṣṇa presented salutation to his own other form and exclaimed, “How wonderful! Lo! This mountain has manifested himself.

devanagari: एषोऽवजानतो मर्त्यान् कामरूपी वनौकसः । हन्ति ह्यस्मै नमस्यामः शर्मणे आत्मनो गवाम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣo hanti hyasmai namasyāmaḥ śarmaṇe ātmano gavām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [ablative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [genitive single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[ablative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [genitive single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: This mountain is capable of taking any form he likes, at will. He kills the forest-dwellers who showed disrespect to him. To him let us bow for the welfare of us as well as of our cattle.”

devanagari: इत्यद्रिगोद्विजमखं वासुदेवप्रचोदिताः । यथा विधाय ते गोपा सहकृष्णा व्रजं ययुः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityadrigodvijamakhaṃ vāsudevapracoditāḥ yathā vidhāya te gopā sahakṛṣṇā vrajaṃ yayuḥ
translation:
The Gopas thus carried out the worship of the mountain, the cows and the Brāhmaṇas according to the directions of Lord Vāsudeva and they returned to Vraja along with Kṛṣṇa.
